title: Apply: genetics vocabulary
description: Apply your understanding of genetics vocabulary in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a population of pea plants, some plants have tall stems and others have short stems. The gene for stem height has two possible alleles. One allele is for tall stems (*T*), and the other allele is for short stems (*t*).

Consider this information about a certain pea plant in the population:

* The plant has a tall stem.

* The plant has two alleles for a tall stem.

**What phenotype does this plant have for the stem height trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of pea plants, some plants have yellow seeds and others have green seeds. The gene for seed color has two possible alleles. One allele is for yellow seeds (*Y*), and the other allele is for green seeds (*y*).

Consider this information about a certain pea plant in the population:

* The plant has yellow seeds.

* The plant has one allele for yellow seeds, and one allele for green seeds.

**What phenotype does this plant have for the seed color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of *Hibiscus* plants, some individuals have red flowers and others have white flowers. The gene for flower color has two possible alleles. One allele is for red flowers (*F*), and the other allele is for white flowers (*f*).

Consider this information about a certain plant in the population:

* The plant has white flowers.

* The plant has two alleles for white flowers.

**What genotype does this plant have for the flower color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of jaguars, some individuals have a black coat and others have a spotted coat. The gene for coat type has two possible alleles. One allele is for a black coat (*B*), and the other allele is for a spotted coat (*b*).

A certain jaguar in the population has the genotype *BB* for the coat type trait.

**Which term best describes this jaguar’s genotype?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of cats, some individuals have short fur and others have long fur. The gene for fur length has two possible alleles. One allele is for short fur (*F*), and the other allele is for long fur (*f*).

A certain cat in the population has the genotype *Ff* for the fur length trait.

**Which term best describes this cat’s genotype?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of cows, some individuals have a black coat and others have a red coat. The gene for coat color has two possible alleles. One allele is for a black coat (*B*), and the other allele is for a red coat (*b*).

A certain cow in the population has the genotype *bb* for the coat color trait.

**Which term best describes this cow’s genotype?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of snails, some individuals have solid shells, and others have striped shells. The gene for shell type has two possible alleles. One allele is for a solid shell (*B*), and the other allele is for a striped shell (*b*).

A certain snail in the population has the genotype *BB* for the shell type trait.

**Given this information, what phenotype does this snail have for the shell type trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of catfish, some fish have a brown body and others have a white body. The gene for body color has two possible alleles. One allele is for a brown body (*B*), and the other allele is for a white body (*b*).

A certain catfish in the population has the genotype *Bb* for the body color trait.

**Given this information, what phenotype does this catfish have for the body color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of koi fish, some individuals have red eyes and others have black eyes. The gene for eye color has two possible alleles. One allele is for red eyes (*E*), and the other allele is for black eyes (*e*).

A certain koi fish in the population has the genotype *ee* for the eye color trait.

**Given this information, what phenotype does this koi fish have for the eye color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of hamsters, some individuals have straight fur and others have wavy fur. The gene for fur texture has two possible alleles. One allele is for straight fur (*F*), and the other allele is for wavy fur (*f*).

A certain hamster in the population has straight fur.

**Which of these could be the hamster’s genotype for the fur texture trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of chickens, some individuals have white legs and others have yellow legs. The gene for leg color has two possible alleles. One allele is for white legs (*W*), and the other allele is for yellow legs (*w*).

A certain chicken in the population has yellow legs.

**Which of these could be the chicken’s genotype for the leg color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In a population of dogs, some individuals have black fur and others have brown fur. The gene for fur color has two possible alleles. One allele is for black fur (*F*), and the other allele is for brown fur (*f*).

A certain dog in the population has brown fur.

**Which of these could be the dog’s genotype for the fur color trait?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
